body_txt: A character learns that they're adopted and doesn't take it very well. Maybe they feel they've been lied to or maybe they worry about not being a "real part of the family". Either way, their feelings towards their parents become sour due to the fact they're adopted. The reveal of " I Am Not Your Father " and cries of " You're Not My Father !" are extremely common in these sorts of situations. Other situations where a character dislikes their adopted parents, hates the fact they're adopted, or hates their to-be adoptive parents are allowed. If they had already lived a portion of their life with biological parents or other relatives, and only later did something occur to cause them to be adopted by people living elsewhere, perhaps they will try returning to their homeland/birthplace by going on The Homeward Journey . In more unfortunate cases, they may find themselves a Stranger in a Familiar Land . In more fortunate cases, perhaps they will experience a Childhood Friend Romance . Inverse of Happily Adopted . If the discovery of being adopted leads to the adoptee searching for their biological parents, that's Gene Hunting . If they find their biological parents and a fight breaks out over who's really the character's parent, see Adoption Conflict . Compare and contrast Bastard Angst (where the child angsts about being born out of wedlock), Orphan's Ordeal and Adoption Diss . Overlaps with Not Blood, Not Family if an adopted character insists that their adopted family isn't their "real" family.
